args.content: Issue-driven architecture remediation for persistent sidepanel transcript disappearance and streaming slowness. The approved design changes the sidepanel from render-time whole-transcript reprojection to an OpenCode-aligned stable transcript pipeline: immutable historical session snapshot plus one mutable live tail, incremental event application, stable message/part identity, and renderer consumption of a precomputed read model. Existing ApplicationComponents remain authoritative: ELM-APP-EXT-CONVERSATION-LIVE-HISTORY-UX owns history/session boundaries, ELM-APP-EXT-RUN-CONVERSATION-MAPPER owns transcript semantic merge logic, and ELM-APP-EXT-CONVERSATION-RENDERER owns display only. The implementation must introduce a dedicated Sidepanel Streaming Transcript Projection software unit instead of another localized patch.

result.designSummary: Issue-driven architecture remediation for persistent sidepanel transcript disappearance and streaming slowness. The approved design changes the sidepanel from render-time whole-transcript reprojection to an OpenCode-aligned stable transcript pipeline: immutable historical session snapshot plus one mutable live tail, incremental event application, stable message/part identity, and renderer consumption of a precomputed read model. Existing ApplicationComponents remain authoritative: ELM-APP-EXT-CONVERSATION-LIVE-HISTORY-UX owns history/session boundaries, ELM-APP-EXT-RUN-CONVERSATION-MAPPER owns transcript semantic merge logic, and ELM-APP-EXT-CONVERSATION-RENDERER owns display only. The implementation must introduce a dedicated Sidepanel Streaming Transcript Projection software unit instead of another localized patch.

args.content: ADR: Persistent sidepanel transcript disappearance/slowness is a structural rendering-pipeline issue, not a localized renderer bug. Rejected continuing with view-only patches because ReasoningTimeline still rebuilds transcript parts from all runSegments during render and the controller still causes high-frequency session/history churn during streaming.

decisions: ADR: Persistent sidepanel transcript disappearance/slowness is a structural rendering-pipeline issue, not a localized renderer bug. Rejected continuing with view-only patches because ReasoningTimeline still rebuilds transcript parts from all runSegments during render and the controller still causes high-frequency session/history churn during streaming.

args.content: ADR: Introduce software unit SU-EXT-STREAMING-TRANSCRIPT-PROJECTION (Sidepanel Streaming Transcript Projection) as an internal decomposition of ELM-APP-EXT-RUN-CONVERSATION-MAPPER. This unit owns a stable transcript read model composed from immutable historical transcript content and one mutable live tail, with incremental append/replace semantics and stable message/part ids.

decisions: ADR: Persistent sidepanel transcript disappearance/slowness is a structural rendering-pipeline issue, not a localized renderer bug. args.content: ADR: Keep extension/src/sidepanel/reasoningTimeline.ts as the semantic authority, but split batch historical projection from incremental live projection. Historical runs may still use batch builders for archive/test views; active runs must use incremental event application instead of replaying all history on every stream event.

Stable keys/identity and tail-only updates are mandatory to align with OpenCode-style client behavior.

args.title: Live transcript projection remains batch-rebuilt in commit d848fde3b6d2fb9c5cf022b299f42332d753bd40
args.content: Commit d848fde3b6d2fb9c5cf022b299f42332d753bd40 partially remediates the sidepanel transcript pipeline but does not fully satisfy the approved structural design for TASK-095/TASK-096. The code freezes historical transcript content and renders from a TranscriptReadModel, yet live updates still derive the tail by rebuilding from the entire live event array rather than incrementally applying deltas/replacements to one mutable live tail. Software-unit-level ArchitectureID trace evidence is also absent for SU-EXT-STREAMING-TRANSCRIPT-PROJECTION, SU-EXT-LIVE-HISTORY-WORKSPACE, and SU-EXT-CONVERSATION-RENDERER.

args.content: Audit passed for rework commit 40bb7b1bc744f6e9a5283cbc59ad578643933572 covering TASK-095,TASK-096,TASK-099 against the approved sidepanel transcript pipeline baseline. The live transcript path now reuses prior liveProjectionState, applies only delta events via options.events.slice(liveState.eventCount), and falls back to rebuild only when the live event/answer prefix changes. Historical transcript snapshot reuse, single mutable live tail, stable ids/keys, renderer consumption of transcriptReadModel, and streaming-safe history freezing remain aligned with intent. Non-blocking traceability gap remains at software-unit marker granularity for SU-EXT-STREAMING-TRANSCRIPT-PROJECTION, SU-EXT-LIVE-HISTORY-WORKSPACE, and SU-EXT-CONVERSATION-RENDERER.

args.title: Formal Requirement
args.content:
# Formal Requirement Draft

## Goal
Preserve enough OpenCode server event information across the Python adapter → extension boundary so the extension can correctly assemble a coherent chat/session transcript, while improving transcript UX by suppressing direct display of TOOL items and aggregating TEXT content into user-visible conversational messages.

## Scope
This requirement applies to:
- The Python adapter that normalizes OpenCode server events into extension-facing `NormalizedRunEvent` items.
- The extension-side transcript projection logic.
- The sidepanel transcript rendering policy.

This requirement does **not** require:
- New end-user-visible tooling output rendering in this change.
- Changes to unrelated event types or unrelated UI surfaces unless they consume the same transcript projection contract.

## Backward Compatibility & Resolved Conflicts
- **Historical context check**: No relevant prior `Requirement`, `Constraint`, or `BusinessProcess` items were found in the architecture graph for this topic.
- **Conflict analysis**: No explicit historical conflicts were detected.
- **Compatibility intent**:
  - Existing adapter normalization may continue, but it must no longer discard information that is needed for downstream transcript assembly.
  - TOOL-type events may still cross the adapter boundary for internal processing, state derivation, or future use, but they must not be rendered as direct transcript messages in the conversation UI.
  - TEXT-type streaming/chunked content must be combined into coherent displayed messages rather than shown as one message per chunk.

## User Stories / Usage Scenarios
1. **As a user**, I want the conversation transcript to read like a normal chat, not like a low-level stream of fragmented event chunks.
2. **As a user**, I do not want raw TOOL activity displayed as if it were conversational content.
3. **As the extension transcript layer**, I need enough preserved server-side event detail from the adapter to reconstruct and project the conversation accurately.
4. **As a future implementer**, I need the adapter/extension contract to preserve semantically important fields even if the current UI does not display all of them.

## Acceptance Criteria (Mandatory)
1. **Information preservation across adapter boundary**
   - Given the OpenCode server emits event data containing information needed to reconstruct the conversation/session transcript,
   - When the Python adapter produces `NormalizedRunEvent` items for the extension,
   - Then the adapter must preserve that key transcript-assembly information rather than dropping it during normalization.
   - And the preserved information must remain available to extension-side projection logic even if some parts are not directly rendered to the user.

2. **TOOL items are not directly displayed**
   - Given the extension transcript projection receives TOOL-type items,
   - When the sidepanel conversation UI assembles display messages,
   - Then TOOL-type items must not be rendered directly as transcript/chat messages.

3. **TEXT chunks are aggregated**
   - Given the extension transcript projection receives multiple TEXT-type items or chunks that belong to the same logical conversational message,
   - When the UI assembles the transcript,
   - Then those TEXT items must be merged into a single displayed message rather than displayed as separate chunk-level messages.

4. **Projection logic, not raw transport order, determines display grouping**
   - Given a stream of normalized events from the adapter,
   - When the extension builds the displayed transcript,
   - Then the displayed conversation must be based on projection/assembly rules that group related TEXT content into coherent messages and exclude TOOL items from direct display.

5. **No regression in transcript completeness**
   - Given a session containing both TOOL and TEXT events,
   - When the transcript is assembled for the conversation UI,
   - Then user-visible conversational content must remain complete and understandable, and required context must not be lost because of adapter-side field omission.

## Constraints & Non-functional Requirements
- The adapter → extension contract semantics are part of scope and may need revision to explicitly preserve transcript-relevant fields.
- The UI must favor readability and conversational coherence over raw event-by-event fidelity.
- Hidden TOOL items may still be retained internally for state, traceability, debugging, or future transcript features; this requirement only forbids direct display in the conversation UI.
- Aggregation behavior must be deterministic so the same event stream yields the same projected transcript.
- This change is architecture-affecting because it alters cross-boundary data preservation expectations and transcript assembly policy.

## Open Questions / Assumptions
- **Assumption**: “Key information” means any event data required by extension logic to reconstruct a correct conversational transcript, even if not all of that data is displayed.
- **Assumption**: TEXT aggregation should occur at the logical message level, not across separate conversational turns.
- **Open implementation detail for architecture/design**: the exact preserved fields and the exact grouping boundary rules still need system design definition, but the product requirement is that transcript-relevant information must not be lost and chunk-level TEXT must not be exposed as separate displayed messages.

args.title: Opencode Event Normalizer
args.content: Describe the element as an application component: a modular application building block that encapsulates automated behavior and data. In this architecture, Opencode Event Normalizer encapsulates the Python adapter logic that converts opencode SSE payloads into normalized run events while preserving transcript-relevant message identity, part identity, channel semantics, emission kind, and compact tool metadata required for downstream transcript assembly.

In this architecture, Opencode Event Normalizer encapsulates the Python adapter logic that converts opencode SSE payloads into normalized run events while preserving transcript-relevant message identity, part identity, channel semantics, emission kind, and compact tool metadata required for downstream transcript assembly.

args.title: Extension Stream Contract
args.content: Describe the element as an application component: a modular application building block that encapsulates automated behavior and data. In this architecture, Extension Stream Contract owns the shared TypeScript protocol and runtime schema validation for normalized run events consumed by the browser extension, including transcript-preserving semantic identifiers and internal-only metadata needed by projection logic without forcing all preserved fields into the visible chat surface.

documentation.value: Describe the element as an application component: a modular application building block that encapsulates automated behavior and data. In this architecture, Extension Stream Contract owns the shared TypeScript protocol and runtime schema validation for normalized run events consumed by the browser extension, including transcript-preserving semantic identifiers and internal-only metadata needed by projection logic without forcing all preserved fields into the visible chat surface.

args.title: Reasoning Projection Mapper
args.content: Describe the element as an application component: a modular application building block that encapsulates automated behavior and data. In this architecture, Reasoning Projection Mapper builds the read-model for assistant text, questions, reasoning, and error turns from normalized run events and is the single authority for grouping text by stable logical message identity while suppressing TOOL items from direct conversation rendering.

documentation.value: Describe the element as an application component: a modular application building block that encapsulates automated behavior and data. In this architecture, Reasoning Projection Mapper builds the read-model for assistant text, questions, reasoning, and error turns from normalized run events and is the single authority for grouping text by stable logical message identity while suppressing TOOL items from direct conversation rendering.

args.title: Conversation Renderer
args.content: Describe the element as an application component: a modular application building block that encapsulates automated behavior and data. In this architecture, Conversation Renderer renders only the already-projected conversation transcript and interaction affordances supplied by the projection mapper, without reinterpreting raw tool events or redefining message-grouping policy.

documentation.value: Describe the element as an application component: a modular application building block that encapsulates automated behavior and data. In this architecture, Conversation Renderer renders only the already-projected conversation transcript and interaction affordances supplied by the projection mapper, without reinterpreting raw tool events or redefining message-grouping policy.

args.content: ELM-006 remediation is a structural application-layer refinement across the Python adapter, shared extension protocol, transcript projection mapper, and conversation renderer. The adapter must preserve transcript-relevant message identity, part identity, channel semantics, emission kind, and compact tool metadata across the NormalizedRunEvent boundary. The extension contract must expose those semantics without promoting all preserved metadata to visible UI content. The projection mapper becomes the single authority for transcript assembly: TOOL items remain internally available but are suppressed from direct conversation rendering, while TEXT-bearing assistant items are merged by stable logical message identity so one assistant message renders once. Existing modules provide the correct ownership boundaries; no new software unit is required.

result.designSummary: ELM-006 remediation is a structural application-layer refinement across the Python adapter, shared extension protocol, transcript projection mapper, and conversation renderer. The adapter must preserve transcript-relevant message identity, part identity, channel semantics, emission kind, and compact tool metadata across the NormalizedRunEvent boundary. The extension contract must expose those semantics without promoting all preserved metadata to visible UI content. The projection mapper becomes the single authority for transcript assembly: TOOL items remain internally available but are suppressed from direct conversation rendering, while TEXT-bearing assistant items are merged by stable logical message identity so one assistant message renders once. Existing modules provide the correct ownership boundaries; no new software unit is required.

args.content: ADR-ELM-006-01: Extend existing software units instead of creating a new transcript module. Rationale: python_adapter/app/opencode_adapter.py and extension/src/sidepanel/reasoningTimeline.ts already own normalization and projection semantics, so adding a new unit would duplicate existing boundaries rather than improve separation of concerns.

decisions: ADR-ELM-006-01: Extend existing software units instead of creating a new transcript module. Rationale: python_adapter/app/opencode_adapter.py and extension/src/sidepanel/reasoningTimeline.ts already own normalization and projection semantics, so adding a new unit would duplicate existing boundaries rather than improve separation of concerns.

args.content: ADR-ELM-006-02: Use semantic.messageId as the primary logical assistant-message anchor and semantic.partId as the secondary chunk/part anchor. Rationale: current projection logic already partially relies on message identity, but deterministic one-message rendering requires the adapter contract to preserve stable server-side logical message identity across delta, snapshot, and final emissions.

Rationale: current projection logic already partially relies on message identity, but deterministic one-message rendering requires the adapter contract to preserve stable server-side logical message identity across delta, snapshot, and final emissions.

args.content: ADR-ELM-006-03: Preserve TOOL metadata across the adapter boundary but suppress TOOL items from direct conversation rendering. Rationale: the requirement forbids direct TOOL display in the conversation UI, but transcript/session assembly and future diagnostics still need compact tool state and identity metadata.

Rationale: the requirement forbids direct TOOL display in the conversation UI, but transcript/session assembly and future diagnostics still need compact tool state and identity metadata.

args.content: ADR-ELM-006-04: Keep transcript grouping and suppression policy inside the Reasoning Projection Mapper, not inside JSX rendering components. Rationale: grouping by logical message identity and hiding TOOL items are application behavior concerns; the renderer should consume the projected read-model without reinterpreting raw event semantics.

Rationale: grouping by logical message identity and hiding TOOL items are application behavior concerns; the renderer should consume the projected read-model without reinterpreting raw event semantics.

tasks.title: Implement adapter transcript-preserving normalized event semantics in python_adapter/app

args.content: Issue-driven defect analysis for duplicated/split assistant transcript output. Repository inspection shows the primary root cause is in extension/src/sidepanel/reasoningTimeline.ts within the Reasoning Projection Mapper (ELM-APP-EXT-RUN-CONVERSATION-MAPPER). The mapper currently segments assistant_output fragments by stream interruptions such as tool/question boundaries and later appends new visible assistant text parts for the same semantic messageId when more assistant_text delta/snapshot/final events arrive. This produces malformed fragment boundaries and visible duplicate final answers. The renderer is not the policy owner and python_adapter/app/opencode_adapter.py already preserves transcript-relevant semantic identity, messageId, partId, and emissionKind contract sufficiently for downstream merge/replace logic. Therefore the fix is implementation-scoped to the extension projection mapper plus regression coverage; adapter changes are not required unless implementation discovers a concrete raw-event counterexample.

The mapper currently segments assistant_output fragments by stream interruptions such as tool/question boundaries and later appends new visible assistant text parts for the same semantic messageId when more assistant_text delta/snapshot/final events arrive. This produces malformed fragment boundaries and visible duplicate final answers. The renderer is not the policy owner and python_adapter/app/opencode_adapter.py already preserves transcript-relevant semantic identity, messageId, partId, and emissionKind contract sufficiently for downstream merge/replace logic. Therefore the fix is implementation-scoped to the extension projection mapper plus regression coverage; adapter changes are not required unless implementation discovers a concrete raw-event counterexample.

args.content: ADR: Assign transcript semantic coalescing and replacement policy to Reasoning Projection Mapper (ELM-APP-EXT-RUN-CONVERSATION-MAPPER). Fixes must ensure one visible assistant transcript message per logical assistant messageId and that snapshot/final updates replace or absorb prior partial output instead of appending an extra visible text fragment.

args.content: Architecture compliance audit passed for commit f0652e7 covering TASK-111 and TASK-112. Reviewed diff shows implementation localized to extension/src/sidepanel/reasoningTimeline.ts plus regression tests in extension/src/sidepanel/reasoningTimeline.test.ts, matching ELM-APP-EXT-RUN-CONVERSATION-MAPPER intent. Fix reuses assistant_output items by logical assistant message groupAnchorId/messageId across tool/question interruptions and replaces merged transcript text for delta/snapshot/final/result updates, preventing duplicate final output. No python_adapter changes and no renderer policy changes were introduced, aligning with approved ADR scope. Automated tests include @ArchitectureID: ELM-APP-EXT-RUN-CONVERSATION-MAPPER and cover split fragments, tool-interleaved continuity, and duplicate final suppression; verified intent coverage satisfies the linked architecture element for this batch.
